message:
fix(scanner): parallelize /proc scan, optimize walker hot path for production workloads

loop_main.rs:
- Move collect_open_path_ancestors() to background thread that runs
  in parallel with the walker's initial directory reads, then lazy-join
  on first classified entry — eliminates ~5s scanner thread blocking

scanner/walker.rs:
- Single-stat optimization: stat directory once at entry, reuse for
  both cross-device guard and WalkEntry emission (saves 1 syscall/dir)
- Detect .sbh-protect markers during child iteration instead of
  separate lstat before read_dir (saves 1 syscall/dir)
- Replace send_timeout(100ms) with try_send() for child dispatch:
  send_timeout caused catastrophic slowdown on 60K+ child directories
  where every overflow child waited 100ms (60K * 100ms = 100 minutes)
- Defer cross-device check to child processing time instead of
  per-child stat in parent (mount points caught at top of fn)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
